svg.text: Eviction Policy Trade-offs — LRU vs LFU vs FIFO vs ARC vs TinyLFU
Six-dimensional comparison and decision framework for policy selection
* LFU with frequency buckets (not min-heap) achieves O(1)
Policy Deep Dive — How Each Works Internally
Access moves item to MRU end.
Maintains 4 lists: T1 (recent, once), T2 (frequent), B1 (ghost of T1), B2 (ghost of T2).
Ghost lists track evicted keys' metadata (not data) to learn access patterns.
Adaptively sizes T1 vs T2 using parameter p based on B1/B2 hit ratios.
Used in: ZFS ARC, database buffer pools, enterprise storage.
Uses Count-Min Sketch (probabilistic frequency counter) to estimate access frequency.
W-TinyLFU: small LRU window for new items, SLRU main space for probation/resident.
Admission gate: new item admitted only if its CMS frequency exceeds resident tail.
Used in: Caffeine (Java), Redis (as module), many modern in-process caches.
Best overall hit rate, low memory
Decision Framework — How to Choose the Right Policy
Q1: Is memory overhead a primary concern?
Q2A: Do you need scan resistance?
Low memory, simple, good enough for most workloads
Q2B: Is access pattern highly skewed (Zipfian)?
LFU if simplicity needed; TinyLFU for best overall
Q3: Do you need self-tuning for mixed workloads?
Self-tunes between recency and frequency automatically
Simple, well-understood, good default for temporal locality
For most production systems, TinyLFU (via Caffeine) or approximated LRU (via Redis) are the top choices.
